text1:
Constructed using advanced pre-Collapse industrial techniques within the shipyards of Kazeron, this warship was commissioned directly by the ruling Gens of the Persean League. Its uncanny resemblance to extinct Mayasuran naval designs was initially flagged by historians-only for those same scholars to later retract their claims without explanation. Now bearing the colors of the Persean League, this vessel embodies the Navarchy's doctrine of decisive speed and overwhelming force, equipped with radically retrofitted engines using pre-Collapse engineering.
The Accipiter-class destroyer is a light combat carrier with enough armament and defensive capability that it can wade directly into battle. Being able to fight comes at the cost of carrying capacity. As such the Accipiter only brings one wing of fighters to the field though it can sortie reserve craft to briefly increase its strike capabilities.

Constructed using advanced pre-Collapse industrial techniques within the shipyards of Kazeron, this warship was commissioned directly by the ruling Gens of the Persean League. Now bearing the colors of the Persean League, this vessel embodies the Navarchy's doctrine of decisive speed and overwhelming force, equipped with radically retrofitted engines using pre-Collapse engineering.
The Buzzard-class is an enigma in Naval classification. Not quite a light cruiser but not quite a full line cruiser either, the Buzzard packs potent firepower into a mobile hull but it some what lacks defensive capability compared with a proper cruiser like the Eagle.

The Gladius (PL) serves as yet another instance of the Gens of Kazeron's efforts to assert superiority among less venerable member worlds. Boasting enhanced engines and a combat shield generator, the Gens promotes it as an evolution of the standard Gladius fighter, conveniently omitting any mention of its significantly lighter armor.

While carrier-based aircraft may not typically hold a prominent role within the Persean League, the Gens of Kazron broke from convention by forming its own 'elite' fighter squadrons. An illustrative case is the Thunder (PL), featuring upgraded engines and a compact shield generator. However, this comes at the trade-off of replacing its hull with lighter armor, placing the onus on the craft to demonstrate its worth.
